factors.summary: North American demand is the fastest-growing end market, particularly in commercial rental.

evidence.text: Commercial rental performance improved in the second quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: North American demand growth and higher pricing indicate a positive trend.

factors.summary: Cost actions, such as residual value policy changes, are delivering savings.

evidence.text: Results benefited from $10.0 million of lower depreciation in the second quarter of 2015 due to residual value policy changes implemented January 1, 2015 as well as growth in average lease and rental fleet size.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost savings from policy changes show positive margin improvement.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable, with no significant risks of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: NYE units increased 50% compared to June 30, 2014, due to new lease sales activity. NLE units decreased compared to June 30, 2014, reflecting lower used vehicle inventories.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory stability with mixed unit changes shows no clear trend.

factors.summary: Management is confident in future growth, particularly in the FMS segment.

evidence.text: We expect favorable full service lease revenue comparisons to continue through the end of the year based on strong sales activity. Commercial rental performance improved in the second quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence in growth and strong sales activity is very positive.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign exchange impacts and lower fuel prices.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth in the second quarter of 2015 by 200 basis points. In the first half of 2015, foreign exchange negatively impacted total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points and 200 basis points, respectively.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and fuel price impacts pose significant risks.

evidence.text: Commercial rental performance improved in the second quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable economic conditions with moderate growth are neutral.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange is negatively impacting revenue growth, particularly in the first half of 2015.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth in the second quarter of 2015 by 200 basis points. In the first half of 2015, foreign exchange negatively impacted total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points and 200 basis points, respectively.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange has significantly negatively impacted revenue growth, particularly in the first half of 2015.

factors.summary: Demand is showing a positive trend, with acceleration across all business segments and particularly strong growth in the full service lease and commercial rental segments.

evidence.text: Operating revenue increased 6% in the second quarter and 5% in the first half of 2015, driven by growth in all business segments.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue increased 8% in both the second quarter and the first half of 2015 reflecting higher North American demand and pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is showing a positive trend with acceleration across all business segments, especially in full service lease and commercial rental.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, with evidence of successful price increases across both lease and rental segments. Commercial rental pricing specifically showed a 4% increase in the second quarter and first half of 2015, driven by higher demand and strategic pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: Higher prices on full service lease vehicles and increased rental demand contributed to revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: Lease and Rental

evidence.text: Commercial rental performance improved in the second quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing. Results benefited from $10.0 million of lower depreciation in the second quarter of 2015 due to residual value policy changes implemented January 1, 2015 as well as growth in average lease and rental fleet size.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company demonstrates strong pricing power with successful price increases across lease and rental segments, driven by higher demand and strategic adjustments.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in commercial rental and full service lease, driven by higher pricing and increased demand.

evidence.text: Lease and rental gross margin increased 10% in the second quarter and 8% in the first half of 2015, driven by higher per-vehicle pricing.
evidence.subsection: Lease and Rental

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue increased 8% in both the second quarter and the first half of 2015 reflecting higher North American demand and pricing. Full service lease revenue increased 5% in both the second quarter and the first half of 2015 due to growth in the fleet size and higher prices on replacement vehicles.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, driven by higher pricing and increased demand.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by new business and higher volumes, particularly in the full service lease and commercial rental product lines.

evidence.text: Operating revenue growth was 6% in the second quarter and 5% in the first half of 2015, outpacing prior periods.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding fuel) increased 6% in the second quarter and 5% in the first half of 2015 as a result of organic growth primarily in the full service lease and commercial rental product lines.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by new business and higher volumes in key product lines.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments, including full service lease and commercial rental. The growth is supported by increased demand and higher utilization rates.

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue grew due to increased North American demand and higher rental pricing.
evidence.subsection: Lease and Rental

evidence.text: The average number of full service lease vehicles increased 4% from the prior year, reflecting continued strong sales activity. Commercial rental utilization — power fleet (3) 78.1% 78.3% 75.8% 76.0% (20) bps (20) bps
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments, supported by increased demand and higher utilization rates.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel costs, are declining due to lower fuel prices. This decline is evident across multiple areas of the business, contributing to revenue changes in related services.

evidence.text: Lower fuel prices passed through to customers contributed to revenue declines in fuel services.
evidence.subsection: Fuel
Dedicated Transportation Solutions

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining input costs, particularly fuel costs, are contributing to revenue changes in related services.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are key sensitivities that have negatively impacted revenue growth. Both factors were highlighted as significant in affecting financial performance, with foreign exchange specifically impacting revenue growth by 200 basis points in both the second quarter and first half of 2015.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted revenue growth by 200 basis points in both the second quarter and first half of 2015.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Fuel services revenue is an ancillary service that we provide our customers and is impacted by fluctuations in market fuel prices. Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth in the second quarter of 2015 by 200 basis points.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are key sensitivities that have significantly negatively impacted revenue growth.

factors.summary: Margins are improving across segments, with lease and rental gross margins expanding to 32% in the second quarter and 30% in the first half of 2015, and FMS segment margins also improving due to higher revenue and cost management.

evidence.text: Lease and rental gross margin increased to 32% in the second quarter and 30% in the first half of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Lease and Rental

evidence.text: FMS EBT increased 8% in the second quarter of 2015 to $122.5 million reflecting higher full service lease results and strong commercial rental performance, partially offset by higher spending on strategic investments primarily in sales and marketing and technology, in order to accelerate growth.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are improving across segments, with lease and rental gross margins expanding and FMS segment margins also improving due to higher revenue and cost management.
